The ROCm 7.2 Stage-B large 1-GPU job still used two partitions, each estimated at 2,859 seconds—longer than the 45-minute step timeout. The default AMD workflow already splits the same suite into three partitions.

Motivation
reasoning_effort accepts the OpenAI string tiers (none/minimal/low/medium/high/xhigh/max, string-only per the spec) plus an sglang float extension in [0.0, 0.99]. The schema had drifted across the surfaces that take it:

/v1/responses (ResponseReasoningParam.effort) only listed low/medium

sglang.title: Fix dropped Inkling reasoning at stream end
sglang.author: ispobock
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
With stream_reasoning=False, the Inkling reasoning detector buffers the thinking block into _pending_reasoning and only flushes it when a control/end token closes the block. When generation is cut mid-block (e.g. max_tokens truncates a <|content_thinking|> block), the stream ends with no

sglang.title: [Diffusion] msgpack raw-bytes transport (drop base64/JSON)
sglang.author: Rockdu
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The rollout /generate endpoint serialized tensor payloads as base64 strings inside a JSON response. Because JSON is text-only, raw tensor bytes had to be base64-encoded (~33% size inflation) and paid base64 encode/decode CPU on both ends, on top of JSON serialize/parse cost for large tens

python3 -m py_compile python/sglang/srt/model

On Ascend NPU, the per-step attention preprocessing in the LLaDA2 MoE model (splitting the fused QKV projection, applying QK RMSNorm, and applying RoPE) is executed as several separate ops, which adds kernel-launch overhead.
sgl_kernel_npu provides a fused kernel, split_qkv_rmsnorm_rope_p

SGLang allocates the FlashInfer MoE result in symmetric memory, but older FlashInfer releases did not let the monolithic trtllm_fp8_block_scale_moe API write into that buffer. The result therefore required an additional copy_, and the first version of this PR used a dual-stream copy/add w

Qwen VLM preprocessing currently runs synchronously on the tokenizer event loop. A burst of image requests therefore serializes image/video loading, Hugging Face processor work, and request dispatch before the GPU scheduler can form a useful prefill batch.
This PR:

gives multimodal I/O and

Run existing stop-string and stop-token checks before using grammar termination as the finish fallback. This preserves the earliest registered stop boundary when speculative decoding accepts multiple tokens.
Root cause
Previously, Req.update_finish_state used this ordering:
max length
-> gra

Chunked-prefix MHA needs per-token LSE to merge suffix and cached-prefix attention states. The piecewise CUDA-graph custom op exposed only attention output and inferred MHA identity from HIP-specific state.

RL weight updates re-run layer post-processing after model initialization.
Those paths may call get_current_vllm_config(), but the worker reload APIs did
not establish the config context, causing an assertion failure on the FP8 MoE +
DeepEP path.
This PR wraps the stable worker-level reload

Fix regression introduced in #45026 (ebfbcfe46) where GroupCoordinator.__init__ makes all subgroups inherit device_index from _WORLD.

A single-token prompt has no scored prompt-logprobs positions, and the two model runners represent that case differently: Model Runner V1 returns an empty [0, k+1] tensor that decodes to zero scored positions, while Model Runner V2 omits the payload entirely (gpu/sample/prompt_logprob.py hit

Repro:
CompletionRequest(model="x", prompt="hi

vllm.author: xuanyu-mistral

Purpose
Add routing_replay_out support to the FlashInfer monolithic MoE backend, enabling the RoutedExpertsCapturer to capture expert routing decisions from the monolithic kernel.
Currently, routing capture only works through the modular kernel path via router.set_capture_fn(). The monolithic path f

vllm.title: [Attention][MLA][DCP] Query replication for MLA decode (DeepSeek-V2/R1 + Kimi-K2.5)
vllm.author: sungsooha
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
With Decode Context Parallelism (DCP) the KV cache is sharded across the DCP group, so the standard MLA decode path all-gathers the query across the group every step so each rank can attend its KV shard with the full head set, then LSE-reduces the partials. That all-gather sits on the decode

vllm.title: [Cleanup] Remove unused StructuredOutputRequest.status field
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This was maybe left over from an earlier implementation; it's never used. It just makes the code more difficult to follow!

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Count per-group blocks in get_max_concurrency_for_kv_cache_config
vllm.author: ormandj
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
get_max_concurrency_for_kv_cache_config sums every group's per-request memory and divides once by group 0's page_size_bytes. For packed UniformTypeKVCacheSpecs, page_size_bytes is aggregated over each group's actual members, so that normalization is wrong when aggregate group sizes differ.
T

vllm.title: Fixes non-coalesced HBM access in marlin_int4_fp8_preprocess_kernel_awq
vllm.author: flutist
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
What does this PR do?
Fixes non-coalesced HBM access in marlin_int4_fp8_preprocess_kernel_awq (AWQ int4-fp8 weight preprocessing kernel, model-load cold path).
Problem
Original thread mapping used threadIdx.x for the row dimension:
qweight[(blockIdx.x * 32 + threadIdx.x) * size_n / 8 + blockIdx.y]
A

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Rust Frontend] Handle zero-column logprobs payloads without panicking
vllm.author: FeathBow
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
The engine-core protocol can carry a logprobs payload with zero scored positions: the natural encoding of an empty position list is a pair of [0, 0] tensors, which is exactly what the crate's own WireLogprobs::from_direct produces for Logprobs { positions: [] }. The decoder passes the column

Also changes the output destination for tracing in the Rust workspace to stderr. The final output will still be written directl

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Propagate quant_config to LFM2 ShortConv projections
vllm.author: yuan-alex
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
LFM2 / LFM2-MoE ShortConv decoder layers construct their projection linears (conv.in_proj, conv.out_proj) without the model's quant_config, so on a quantized checkpoint those linears are built with no quantization method. Loading a quantized LFM2 checkpoint — e.g. a ModelOpt NVFP4 export, wh
